code.description: HTTP-style status; mirrors response status code

status.description: Focal post, or a tombstone when the post is unavailable

type.description: Facet kind: e.g. url, mention, hashtag, bold, media, custom_emoji (Mastodon custom emoji image)
Facet kind: e.g. url, mention, hashtag, bold, media, custom_emoji (Mastodon custom emoji image)
Facet kind: e.g. url, mention, hashtag, bold, media, custom_emoji (Mastodon custom emoji image)
Facet kind: e.g. url, mention, hashtag, bold, media, custom_emoji (Mastodon custom emoji image)

screen_name.description: Handle or account id used in permalinks (@user on X).

status.description: Parent post id (X: snowflake; Bluesky: record key).

url.description: Permalink to the parent post when known.

profile_url.description: Permalink to the parent author profile when known.

display_name.description: Display name of the parent author when known.

code.description: HTTP-style status; mirrors response status code

type.description: Facet kind: e.g. url, mention, hashtag, bold, media, custom_emoji (Mastodon custom emoji image)

reason.description: Set to `suspended` when the user is suspended; omitted for plain not found.

id.description: Numeric user id when the upstream payload includes it.

type.description: Discriminator: grouped conversation snippet in a timeline (API v2).

truncated.description: Always false for Bluesky grouped timelines (no native full-thread id list on feed rows).

get.description: Returns one Bluesky post by handle and record key (rkey), in the same envelope as FxTwitter API v2 (`code`, `status`, `thread`, `author`).

parameters.description: Post record key from the `at://` URI
parameters.name: rkey

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

200.description: Thread payload (check `code` for upstream errors mirrored as HTTP status)

503.description: Bluesky upstream timeout or error (post may still exist)

get.description: Returns users who reposted the given post, in the same envelope as FxTwitter `GET /2/status/{id}/reposts` (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.feed.getRepostedBy`: pass the prior `cursor.bottom` as the `cursor` query param. `handle` may be a handle or DID (`did:plc:…`). `cursor.top` is always null.

parameters.description: Post record key from the `at://` URI
parameters.name: rkey

404.description: Post not found or list unavailable

get.description: Returns users who liked the given post, in the same envelope as FxTwitter-style user list results (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.feed.getLikes`: pass the prior `cursor.bottom` as the `cursor` query param. `handle` may be a handle or DID (`did:plc:…`). `cursor.top` is always null.

parameters.description: Post record key from the `at://` URI
parameters.name: rkey

404.description: Post not found or list unavailable

get.description: Returns the post chain for a Bluesky thread, matching the shape of FxTwitter `GET /2/thread/{id}`.

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

503.description: Bluesky upstream timeout or error (post may still exist)

get.description: Returns the focal post, ancestor chain and author self-thread (same as `/2/thread/{handle}/{rkey}` on the first page), plus **direct** replies from other participants. Replies are sorted by `ranking_mode` (default likes). Pagination uses an opaque `cursor` query param (`cursor.bottom` from the prior response); Bluesky `getPostThread` has no native reply cursor, so each page refetches the thread slice. Optional `count` (1–100, default 20) sets the reply page size. Very large reply lists still require a large upstream payload when loading direct replies.

parameters.description: How direct replies are ranked (default: likes). Ignored when `cursor` is set; the cursor carries the mode used for the session.
parameters.name: ranking_mode

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from prior response (`cursor.bottom`)
parameters.name: cursor

parameters.description: Reply page size for the first request (default 20). Subsequent pages use the size embedded in `cursor`.
parameters.name: count

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

200.description: Conversation payload with thread, replies, and pagination cursor

400.description: Invalid path, query parameters, or cursor

503.description: Bluesky upstream timeout or error (post may still exist)

get.description: Search posts via Bluesky `app.bsky.feed.searchPosts`. Response shape matches FxTwitter `GET /2/search` (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). `cursor.top` is always null; pass `cursor.bottom` as the `cursor` query param. `feed=latest` and `feed=top` map to Bluesky `sort`. `feed=media` uses `sort=latest` then keeps posts with image, video, or external link embeds (may return fewer hits than `count`). `lang` is used for inline translation only, not the upstream post-language filter.

parameters.description: Search tab (default latest). `media` is a client-side filter on the current result page (no native Bluesky media tab).
parameters.name: feed

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

get.description: Returns trending topic labels and suggested topic feeds from the Bluesky public AppView, in the same envelope as FxTwitter `GET /2/trends` (`code`, `timeline_type`, `trends`, `cursor`). Each trend’s `context` includes a bsky.app URL to the topic feed when the upstream provides a `link`.

parameters.description: Trend list. `trending` returns Bluesky live topics first, then suggested topic feeds to fill `count`. `suggested` returns only suggested feeds. Upstream: `app.bsky.unspecced.getTrendingTopics` (max 25 rows per request).
parameters.name: type

400.description: Invalid query parameters (e.g. `type` or `count` out of allowed range)

404.description: Trends unavailable or empty upstream list

get.description: Returns profile fields in the same envelope as FxTwitter `GET /2/profile/{handle}` (`code`, `message`, `user`). `handle` may be a handle (e.g. `user.bsky.social`) or a DID (`did:plc:…`).

Returns a page in the same shape as FxTwitter `GET /2/profile/{handle}/followers` (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). `handle` may be a handle or DID. Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.graph.getFollowers`: pass the prior `cursor.bottom` as the `cursor` query param. `cursor.top` is always null.

400.description: Invalid path or query parameters (e.g. `count` out of range)

404.description: Actor not found or list unavailable

get.description: Returns a page in the same shape as FxTwitter `GET /2/profile/{handle}/following` (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). `handle` may be a handle or DID. Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.graph.getFollows`: pass the prior `cursor.bottom` as the `cursor` query param. `cursor.top` is always null.

400.description: Invalid path or query parameters (e.g. `count` out of range)

404.description: Actor not found or list unavailable

get.summary: List posts with media for an actor
get.description: Returns a timeline page in the same shape as FxTwitter `GET /2/profile/{handle}/media` (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). `handle` may be a handle or DID. Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.feed.getAuthorFeed` with `filter=posts_with_media`: `cursor.bottom` is the opaque next-page token (pass as `cursor` query param). `cursor.top` is always null.

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

400.description: Invalid path or query parameters (e.g. `count` out of range)

get.summary: List posts liked by an actor
get.description: Returns a timeline page in the same shape as other FxBluesky profile timelines (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). `handle` may be a handle or DID. Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.feed.getActorLikes`: pass the prior `cursor.bottom` as the `cursor` query param. `cursor.top` is always null. **Note:** Bluesky’s lexicon states this method requires authentication and that `actor` must be the requesting account; the public AppView may return **401** or errors for unauthenticated or cross-actor access—callers should handle `code` 401 accordingly.

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

400.description: Invalid path or query parameters (e.g. `count` out of range)

401.description: Upstream rejected the request (e.g. unauthenticated access to likes)

404.description: Actor not found or list unavailable

get.description: Returns a timeline page in the same shape as FxTwitter `GET /2/profile/{handle}/statuses` (`code`, `results`, `cursor`). `handle` may be a handle or DID. Pagination uses Bluesky `app.bsky.feed.getAuthorFeed`: `cursor.bottom` is the opaque next-page token (pass as `cursor` query param). `cursor.top` is always null—there is no reverse cursor on this upstream endpoint. Optional `since` (Unix time): when used without `cursor`, returns **204 No Content** if no posts are strictly newer than that instant. Values ≥ 1e12 are treated as milliseconds.

parameters.description: Unix timestamp (seconds, or ms if ≥ 1e12). Without `cursor`, 204 if no post is strictly newer than this time.
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: If truthy (`1`, `true`, `yes`, `on`, or empty), include replies (`posts_with_replies` upstream); otherwise `posts_no_replies`.
parameters.name: with_replies

parameters.description: If truthy, return `results` as a mix of `type: "status"` and `type: "thread"` entries (consecutive self-reply chains grouped).
parameters.name: groupthreads

parameters.description: Target language (ISO 639-1 or BCP 47) for inline translation when available
parameters.name: lang

200.description: Timeline page (flat or grouped when `groupthreads` is set)

204.description: No posts newer than `since` (only when `since` is set and `cursor` is omitted; same conditions as 200 otherwise)

400.description: Invalid path or query parameters (e.g. `count` out of range)
